notes.content: 2026-05-19: Product manager approved the reusable final workflow concept. Current process: idea/docs -> clarification -> autoresearch -> SDAR approval -> product/frontend skeleton -> interaction sequence + low-fidelity prototype -> page structure/route/API/data contracts -> small-node implementation -> verification -> acceptance log. Current Phase 1 asset checkpoint is .omx/logs/current-assets-progress-ai-counselor-demo-20260519.md; next approval doc is SDAR-0005 frontend page structure and interaction flow.

directives.directive: When a task has multiple viable alternatives, implementation paths, UI/product expressions, data-contract choices, component choices, or backend boundary options, first present options, tradeoffs, recommendation, risks, and rollback to the product manager for alignment; use Deep Interview-style clarification when ambiguity remains before implementation.

After each completed task node, approval package, verification, or acceptance action, update the project log and the development path structure diagram at .omx/logs/development-path-structure-ai-counselor-demo.md so the current node and next step remain visible.

When presenting animation/effect options for user alignment, always include corresponding visual material for each choice: either self-made demo assets or Autoresearch-discovered finished references. Do not ask the user to choose from text-only descriptions. If creating visual demo material directly, use relevant frontend/design skills to assist development before presenting it.
directives.context: User explicitly requested this during QA answer animation alignment because text-only effect descriptions are hard to understand.

directives.directive: When presenting design/effect options, always include corresponding visual material. If Codex creates the material, use relevant frontend/design/animation skills to build it; do not ask the user to choose from text-only descriptions.
